message: Jake
Ash
Ah.
Are you okay coming to a place like this?
I’m fine, though.
Why's that?
Well, they're all saying that Gai is the culprit, right? So I thought you might be busy with various things.
No, not at all. If Eva were alive, she might have come to my place and poked around.
I hope that’s the case.
Welcome. You've been coming here quite often lately.
Look
Talk
Listen
Think
Move
System

About Cindy
About the Serial Murder Case
About Jennifer

Exit the game
The sounds of angry drunks aren't heard, and a quiet time flows. The music feels a bit loud, but it’s good that I can’t hear the voices of the neighboring customers.
It’s a safe, slightly boring place with no drugs, no prostitutes, and no flashy shows.
Considering it's a front for shady work, the stock of bottles at the back bar is quite abundant.
As usual, he stands in front of me across the counter, listening to my words that could easily be drowned out by the music.
He's a peculiar man who runs a bar at such a young age, and is also connected to the underworld. He might be much more cut out for detective work than I am.
You look downcast. Is it reflecting my mental state? I usually come to this place when something unpleasant happens.
Still, things are getting rough out there.
It’s making me feel gloomy. Two people I recently met were murdered.
That's right, people I've just met have been dying one after another.
I'm going to be a bit busy from now on, too. I might not be able to show my face for a while.
Are you closing the shop?
No, nothing like that.
Well, I’m busy with various things. Let’s just say I’m in a broke and busy state.
I should be careful not to make this my last visit.
That sounds like you might die or something.
I’m fine, but,
just don't go dying somewhere else.
Well, how polite of you, I'm flattered. Heh heh.
Since you came all the way here, forget about the unpleasant stuff.
But it's probably useless to say that.
Yeah.
It’s already stuck in my head and won’t go away.
Are you okay? Please take care.
Even if you tell me not to worry, it's hard, but it's important not to overthink it.
The news about Cindy was shocking, wasn’t it? Is it really okay to talk about things like that?
After all, the media is just like that.
That content sure seemed like it was provoking the culprit.
They were relatively praising Cindy though.
But there was also content that seemed to strike the dead.
That's right, it was as if she had something to do with Guy when she really didn't.
It's harsh to treat someone like this just after they died, especially after being adored until now.
The world is really cold, isn't it?
In the news, it was irresponsibly reported that Guy killed Cindy and potentially all the other women too.
How they could link Guy to the serial murder case is beyond me; the media must be having a headache.
Really.
But it’s not unreasonable to think of Guy as the perpetrator of the series.
When you think about it, at least two women were said to have some connection with Guy, and he’s missing.
Sure, when you say that, it does sound suspicious.
But there's no way Guy would do something like that.
I think so too.
I don't believe he did it.
But I can see how he might look like the criminal.
He hasn't come to this shop since that incident.
Speaking of which, there was a time when she was rumored to be the culprit too.
Everyone is so careless. They quickly label people as criminals.
It's really terrible; they completely disregard the feelings of those involved.
99% of what others say is just gossip.
So it's just nonsense.
Is it a talent to sift through that and pick out the real information?
Well, yeah. There are tons of people making a living off of that.
I don't have that talent. The best approach is to distrust everything anyone says equally.
It's pretty safe, isn't it? The only truth is what you've seen with your own eyes.
It's better not to get swayed by other people's rumors.
Gloria says that the BAM article leaked from the Nets' side.
Is that so!?
I was momentarily in disbelief, but it seems Cindy's side received compensation.
What on earth could they have done that for?
It doesn't make sense; framing Guy does nothing for the Nets.
That guy Guy, he should come out already, considering how things are.
Could he possibly be held captive?
Who would do such a thing?
At the Nets Office.
Isn't that something that just can't happen?
At this rate, they'll pin the blame on the wrong person.
Meryl is in a tough spot, believing Guy is the culprit.
I can kinda imagine how that goes. They probably pressured her to reveal Guy's location.
It's a tough situation since I understand how she feels.
If Guy shows up now, she might pull a knife on him.
At this rate, Meryl might end up being the criminal.
I have to do something about this.
I hope no more unfortunate people get added to this.
I hope so too. At least for the people around me.
We shouldn't take our eyes off Meryl.
Cindy has gone through something tragic.
Who would say they have a reason to kill her?
It doesn't seem like anyone harbors a grudge against her, maybe a fanatic fan? No, not every criminal has a motive.
Cindy's case was breaking news on every network. You could say it provided a perfect story for the media.
There was some sort of reason for killing Eva, but I don't understand why I had to kill Cindy.
Ginny, Eva, Cindy
Could it all be the work of the same person?
Despite all these crimes happening, the police seem completely unable to catch the culprit. The criminal seems to be quite clever.
There are various rumors flying around, but no way it’s Jennifer as the culprit.
That can’t be true, right?
She looks tough on the outside, but to me, she's a kind, ordinary woman.
I can meet her whenever I want, but if the media catches wind of it, it could tarnish Jennifer's reputation.
Even if it was Nez that spread the gossip about Guy, why would they have to do such a thing?
In any case, until I talk to Guy, I won't know what to believe.
Guy is being treated as the suspect.
It's understandable, considering he's been missing for a long time.
But surely he isn't dead somewhere.
If I'd been told sooner that Cindy was my biological daughter, this unfortunate outcome might not have happened.
I just hope I don’t end up stabbing Guy out of frustration.
With Meryl as she is now, she might actually do something like that.
It’s probably best to keep an eye on Meryl for a while.
Next time you come, please bring good news.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
